description: Check the design against what was actually built. You supply what you observed — for each registered artifact, whether it still exists and its current content hash — and reflow2 reports the divergences: files that vanished, files whose content changed since they were registered, and files present but unknown to the design. reflow2 performs no file I/O; compute the hashes yourself (any algorithm, used consistently). The result's `propagation_seeds` are the design nodes the changes land on — feed them to `propagate_from` to see what a code change means upstream.

detected_at.description: Timestamp for recorded events (reflow2 takes no clock).

exhaustive.description:
Assert the observation list is a complete sweep, so registered artifacts
missing from it are reported as unobserved (default false).

observed.description:
What you observed, one entry per artifact you checked:
`{ "artifact_id", "present": bool, "checksum": "<hash>"? }`.

record_events.description: Record a `DriftEvent` per divergence (default false — looking is not writing).

name: reconcile_artifacts
